title: Clutia richardiana
intro: Clutia richardiana is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, commonly known as the Clutia tree. The leaves and roots of Clutia richardiana are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory infections, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Clutia richardiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Clutia richardiana is typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and other health ailments.
taxonomy: Clutia richardiana is classified under the family Phyllanthaceae. This family is a part of the order Malpighiales and class Magnoliopsida. The order Malpighiales is a part of the subclass Rosidae within the class Magnoliopsida. Magnoliopsida is a subclass of the division Magnoliophyta, also known as angiosperms. Clutia richardiana also belongs to the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Clutia richardiana is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal uses.
